data.title: Crypto exec Chris Perkins says industry unaffected if CLARITY Act fails, thanks to SEC and CFTC.
data.description: The actions taken by the chairmen of the SEC and CFTC suggest that the crypto sector may be able to thrive even in the absence of the CLARITY Act, as stated by Chris Perkins, a prominent figure in the cryptocurrency industry.

data.title: Bitcoin records strongest monthly gain in a year during April, slightly below historical average, per CoinGlass data.
data.description: Although it was the top-performing month in the last year, it fell just under its typical average, as per CoinGlass data.

data.title: $15B raised by A16z; Crypto steady - btc $90,600, eth +1% at $3,110; xrp -2% at $2.04; xmr hits new ath $590; Jerome Powell defends vs Trump; Vaneck predicts btc at $53m by 2050; Ripple approved in UK; Bny mellon debuts token deposits; Tether freezes $182m.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies saw mostly flat movement as the market opened on Monday. Bitcoin (BTC) held steady at $90,600, Ethereum (ETH) saw a 1% increase to $3,110, Solana (SOL) rose by 2% to $140, while XRP fell by 2% to $2.04. Top movers included Internet Computer (IP) surging by 20% and Monero (XMR) climbing by 15%, hitting a new all-time high of $590. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell defended himself against criminal charges, attributing them to his resistance in cutting interest rates as per Trump's demands. Twitter announced plans for "smart cashtags" displaying live cryptocurrency and stock prices alongside tickers. Vaneck predicted Bitcoin could hit $53 million by 2050, citing long-term adoption and store-of-value assumptions for a projected 29% annual growth. Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) raised $15 billion to support American Dynamism, focusing on AI and cryptocurrency. Ripple received approval from the Financial Conduct Authority to expand crypto payments in the UK. BNY Mellon introduced tokenized deposits for institutional and digital-native clients. A new house bill aimed to prevent lawmakers and federal officials from engaging in prediction markets. Tether froze $182 million in USDT linked to Venezuela oil trades.
ZEC jumps 11%; Trump not pardoning Sam Bankman-Fried; Florida renews Bitcoin Reserve with btc at $90.3k; eth at $3,090; sol at $138; xrp at $2.10.

data.title: Cryptos declined with BTC below $90K, ZEC dropped 19%. BTC -2% to $89,900; ETH -3% to $3,100; SOL -3% to $134; XRP -7% to $2.08.ZEC's dev team resigned amid a dispute, planning to form a new company to continue. JP Morgan to launch JPM Coin on Canton network. Wyoming issues its first stablecoin, Frontier Stable Token; WLFI, LIT, and XMR saw positive growth. Senate Banking Committee faces pressure as key vote on crypto market structure legislation nears. Barclays invests in UBYX; World Liberty Financial applies for a national trust bank charter to issue/custody its USD1 stablecoin. Starknet experienced multi-hour outage due to a block production bug.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies experienced a decline in value yesterday, with Bitcoin (BTC) dropping by $90,000 to $89,900 (-2%), Ethereum (ETH) falling by 3% to $3,100, Solana (SOL) down by 3% to $134, and XRP plunging by 7% to $2.08. On the other hand, Lit (+3%), WLFI (+3%), and XMR (+3%) were the top gainers for the day. Zcash (ZEC) saw a significant 19% decrease after its developer team resigned due to a dispute with the board, but the team has pledged to establish a new company to continue its mission. JPMorgan revealed intentions to launch its JPM Coin on the Canton network, while Barclays made an investment in UBYX, a U.S. stablecoin settlement startup, to enhance infrastructure for regulated institutions to transfer digital funds. The Senate Banking Committee is under increased pressure leading up to a crucial vote on cryptocurrency market structure legislation next week. Wyoming introduced its inaugural state-backed stablecoin, the Frontier Stable Token, now accessible to the public. World Liberty Financial's subsidiary applied for a national trust bank charter to issue and safeguard its USD1 stablecoin under federal regulation. StarkNet experienced a prolonged outage due to a block production glitch, leading to a network pause and roll back before being restored.

data.title: Crypto majors decline in 2026; BTC at $92k. Morgan Stanley seeks ETFs for BTC, ETH, & SOL. Senate to vote on crypto bill. Hyperliquid stirs airdrop expectations. Nike sells off RTFKT; Clone X surges 250%. ETH records 2M+ daily transactions. Telegram sells $450M TON.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies experienced their first decline in 2026, with Bitcoin (BTC) dropping 2% to $92,000, Ethereum (ETH) falling 1% to $3,210, Solana (SOL) also down 1% at $138, and XRP decreasing by 5% to $2.24. Meanwhile, investment giant Morgan Stanley filed for ETFs related to Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana. In the US, the Senate set a crucial vote on a bill concerning crypto market structure for the upcoming week. Hyperliquid's progress map release sparked speculation about an impending airdrop. Nike announced the sale of RTFKT, while Clone X surged by 250%. Ethereum's network marked a record with over 2 million daily transactions. Telegram disclosed selling $450 million worth of its TON token throughout the previous year.

data.title: Crypto market cap rises to $3.22 trillion in 2026, with BTC at $93,780 and ETH at $3,240. Top gainers include XRP, RENDER, SUI, and LIT. Major banks show increased interest in crypto, while exchanges face security concerns.
data.description: The global cryptocurrency market cap has increased by 2% to reach $3.22 trillion. The leading cryptocurrencies are showing positive momentum with Bitcoin (BTC) rising by 1% to $93,780, Ethereum (ETH) increasing by 2% to $3,240, and Solana (SOL) up by 3% at $139. XRP has surged by 12% to $2.37, with RENDER (+18%), SUI (+18%), and LIT (+15%) leading the top gainers. Bank of America has officially introduced crypto recommendations for wealthy clients, suggesting up to a 4% allocation in portfolios. Morgan Stanley has filed for a Solana Trust with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Goldman Sachs has upgraded Coinbase to a 'Buy' rating while downgrading eToro simultaneously. Japan’s finance minister has expressed support for increased integration of cryptocurrencies, proposing lower taxes and exchange-level reforms. Vitalik Buterin, the co-founder of Ethereum, claimed that Ethereum has effectively resolved the "Blockchain Trilemma" by balancing decentralization and scalability through its Layer-2 roadmap. Kraken is looking into reports of customer data being potentially sold on the dark web following a suspected security breach. Ledger users are on high alert after a data breach involving its e-commerce partner, Global-E, exposed customer contact details.

data.title: Global crypto market cap hits $3.22T. XRP surges 12%. Japan backs crypto. Fear & Greed index at neutral.
data.description: The total market capitalization of the global cryptocurrency market increased by 2% to $3.22 trillion, with leading cryptocurrencies showing positive movements: BTC rose by 1% to $93,780, ETH surged by 2% to $3,240, and SOL jumped by 3% to $139. XRP saw the most significant increase, rising by 12% to $2.37, while RENDER, SUI, and LIT were the top movers with gains of 18%, 18%, and 15%, respectively. Bank of America introduced its crypto investment recommendations for high-net-worth clients, suggesting a portfolio allocation of up to 4%. Morgan Stanley submitted a filing to the SEC for a Solana Trust. Goldman Sachs upgraded Coinbase to a 'Buy' rating while downgrading eToro. Japan's finance minister expressed support for increased crypto integration, proposing lower taxes and reforms at the exchange level. Vitalik Buterin stated that Ethereum has addressed the "Blockchain Trilemma" by achieving a balance between decentralization and scalability through its Layer-2 roadmap. Kraken is looking into reports of customer data being sold on the dark web due to a potential security breach. Ledger users are being vigilant following a data breach involving its e-commerce partner, Global-E, which exposed customer contact information.

data.title: Crypto market cap at $3.16t (+1.5%), btc up 2% at $93k, eth up 1% at $3,175, bnb up 2.5% at $906. Memes surge, Pepe rises 67%, Infinex interview. BTC ETFs receive $471m inflows. SEC commissioner Crenshaw exits. PwC ramps up stablecoin focus.
data.description: The global cryptocurrency market reached a cap of $3.16 trillion, showing a 1.5% increase. Major cryptocurrencies experienced gains, with bitcoin rising by 2% to $93,000, ethereum by 1% to $3,175, Binance Coin by 2.5% to $906, and Solana by 1% to $135. Among the top movers were Virtuals soaring by 24%, Render dropping by 17%, and both BitTorrent Token and Fetch.ai rising by 11%. Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) witnessed $471 million in net inflows on the first trading day of 2026, marking the highest single-day total since November 11. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Commissioner Caroline Crenshaw officially left the agency on January 2nd, resulting in an all-Republican commission. PwC, one of the Big 4 firms, announced an enhanced focus on cryptocurrencies, particularly stablecoins and payment systems.

data.title: Bitget Wallet CMO discusses how crypto wallets can rival neobanks, aiming for a seamless integration of crypto with traditional finance in a vision of "Crypto for Everyone."
data.description: In a recent interview with Decrypt, Jamie Elkaleh, the CMO of Bitget Wallet, discussed the platform's transition from a conventional crypto wallet to a comprehensive everyday finance application. This transformation aims to create a seamless integration between cryptocurrencies and traditional finance (TradFi). Elkaleh emphasized the vision of "Crypto for Everyone" driving this evolution. The platform's conversion reflects a strategic shift toward broadening its financial services and user base, highlighting its commitment to innovation and accessibility.

data.title: Tom Lee buys $130M ETH, holds $1B cash. REKT & WorldStar collab tease. BlackRock BUIDL $100M dividends, $2B assets. Metaplanet holds 35,102 BTC. Unleash Protocol hit by $3.9M exploit. Onchain Perpetuals hit $1T monthly volume. SK's crypto regulation delayed. Mining demand unaffected by BTC price volatility.
data.description: The current market prices are as follows: Bitcoin (BTC) at $87,000 with no change, Bitcoin dominance (btc.D) at 59.0% with no change, Ethereum (ETH) at $2,975 with a 1% increase, Binance Coin (BNB) at $855 with a 1% increase, and Solana (SOL) at $124 with no change. Tom Lee recently purchased an additional $130 million worth of ETH for the holiday season and still retains $1 billion in cash reserves for the upcoming year. REKT drinks and WorldStarHipHop are hinting at a collaboration. BlackRock's BUIDL has surpassed $100 million in dividends and has exceeded $2 billion in assets. Metaplanet has acquired 4,279 bitcoin, increasing their total holdings to 35,102 BTC. Unleash Protocol suffered a $3.9 million exploit, with the funds being funneled through Tornado Cash. Onchain Perpetuals have reached over $1 trillion in monthly volume as traders seek leverage. South Korea is facing delays in crypto regulation, particularly regarding stablecoin rules. The CEO of Abundant Mining stated that despite Bitcoin's price volatility, mining demand remains strong.

data.title: White Whale soars 10x in a week, Saylor buys $109M BTC. Btc: 87k, Eth: 2953, bnb: 853, sol: 124. New TGE buzz. YouTuber exposes daycare fraud, Gold & Silver plunge.
data.description: The latest cryptocurrency market data shows BTC at 87k, experiencing a 1% decrease, with Bitcoin dominance at 59.0%. ETH is currently at 2953, down by 3%, while BNB stands at 853 with a 1% decrease, and SOL is at 124 with a 3% drop. The White Whale has surged almost 15 times in just one week, skyrocketing from a $5 million market capitalization to $71 million. Speculation about a potential Token Generation Event (TGE) is spreading. Additionally, popular YouTuber Nick Shirley exposes widespread fraud within Minnesota's daycare system. On the other hand, both gold and silver prices have seen a significant decline recently.

data.title: Golden Tempo Wins the 152nd Running of the Kentucky Derby Presented by Woodford Reserve
data.description: Record-breaking handle numbers were achieved during Kentucky Derby Week races, setting a new all-time high. The total handle for the week surpassed previous records, with an impressive figure exceeding (insert specific number) million dollars. This milestone marks a significant increase in betting activity compared to past years, solidifying the popularity and success of the Kentucky Derby Week races.

data.title: NomosLogic Founder Matt Hardy Launches Lyceum and Odyssey on Dendrite Lite
data.description: Salt Lake City-based molecular medicine infrastructure company, NomosLogic, Inc., has introduced two new consumer offerings on Dendrite Lite, accessible at lite.dendrite.com. Lyceum is a personalized genetic literacy quiz created from an individual's unique biology. Odyssey encapsulates a user's genetic journey in seven chapters, explained in simple terms for all family members. These features are now live on Theogony, the latest interactive component in Dendrite Lite. This initiative marks a significant step for the company.

data.title: a2 Platinum USA Label Infant Formula Recalled Because of Possible Health Risk
data.description: A recall has been initiated by the a2 Milk Company for three batches of a2 Platinum Premium USA label infant formula 0-12 months due to the presence of cereulide, a heat-stable toxin produced by certain Bacillus cereus strains. The recall only affects products sold in the United States. Illness can result from consuming food contaminated with the toxin, and using hot water to prepare formula does not remove it.

data.title: CISA warns of critical Linux Copy Fail vulnerability permitting root access via 10 lines of Python.
data.description: A cybersecurity researcher has revealed that malicious parties can potentially obtain root access on Linux systems by utilizing just 10 lines of Python code that grants code execution capabilities.

data.title: WIF seen at $0.15 after reaching $0.19 in a bear market rally to $0.21. Final drop predicted due to 200-day moving average.
data.description: The WIF experienced a slight rebound to $0.19, signaling a potential bear market rally to $0.21 before an expected decline. The final drop to $0.15 is anticipated as the gap with the 200-day moving average reaches a critical point.

data.title: PEPE trading at RSI 58 signals potential price consolidation ahead, with breakout or correction expected.
data.description: The cryptocurrency PEPE is currently trading at a Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 58, indicating a state of technical uncertainty with conflicting momentum signals. The current price trend indicates the potential for a breakout above resistance levels or a possible further correction. It is a crucial juncture for PEPE investors as the coin hovers in a state of consolidation. Traders are closely monitoring the RSI and price movements for signals of the next direction.

data.title: ALGO expected to surge to $0.13 before dropping to $0.095 within 10 days, with 70% likelihood of breakdown. Smart money hints at final pump.
data.description: Algorand is showing signs of a potential false breakout, with a 70% chance of reaching $0.095 in the next 10 days. Analysis of smart money positioning indicates a likely final surge to $0.13 before a significant selloff is expected to occur.

data.title: $0.91 FILE in consolidation, may hit $0.88, aiming for $1.20. Smart money sees 65% chance of upside momentum.
data.description: The current price of FILE stands at $0.91, experiencing neutral momentum alongside intense selling activity. There is a focus on reaching the $0.88 support level before a potential substantial increase of 32% to $1.20.
